rawContent: Task: 260409-215133 | Outcome: completed | Observation: Task 260409-215133 (input validation for user registration form) completed all 7 pipeline stages with 0 retries each (~541s duration, full mode). The verify stage reported FAIL with 5 test assertion errors (date formatting mismatches: expected '1/15/2024, 12:30 PM' to contain '10', expected '1/1/2100' to contain '2099') and 4+ lint errors (setState synchronously within effect, no-assign-module-variable), yet the pipeline proceeded through review-fix and ship stages without blocking. The ship stage completed with 0 retries but output 'skipped — local mode, no issue number', indicating no actual shipment occurred — this is the correct behavior for local mode but masks the underlying verify-FAIL-proceeds-to-ship structural flaw. This is the fourteenth consecutive run exhibiting the verify-to-ship gate defect where FAIL status does not prevent pipeline progression. | Pattern: This is the fourteenth consecutive run (832, 841, 260409-111824, 840, 842, 844, two 419-reruns, 847, 846, 858, 863, 874, and now 260409-215133) exhibiting the identical verify-to-ship gate flaw: verify stage reports FAIL due to test failures or type constraint violations, yet the pipeline proceeds through review-fix and ship without blocking. Fourteen prior retrospectives across two days recommended adding a verifyExitCode check in the ship stage template that halts when verify reports FAIL, but no implementation has occurred. Notably, this run's ship stage produced 'skipped' output rather than completing a shipment, which is the correct behavior for local mode but does not resolve the structural gate defect — code that fails verify should not reach ship at all, regardless of whether ship ultimately skips or ships. | Suggestion: Implement the verifyExitCode conditional gate in the ship stage template that was recommended in fourteen prior retrospectives without implementation. This is a configuration change to the ship stage template: add a check that reads the verify stage exit status and halts with an error when verify reports FAIL, preventing code that failed validation from entering the review-fix stage at all. The current approach of relying on ship to skip due to local mode is not a fix — it is coincidental behavior that does not address the structural lack of a gate at the verify-to-ship boundary. | Flaw: verify → ship gate — Verify stage FAIL status does not prevent pipeline progression to ship stage. The pipeline lacks a blocking mechanism at the verify-to-ship boundary. | Stages: taskify:completed, plan:completed, build:completed, verify:completed, review:completed, review-fix:completed, ship:completed
